title: Bridge Paper: Somatosensory coding of visual self-identity.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership as constructed percept.
**Phase context:** body representation, rubber hand illusion, multisensory integration, body ownership, bodily self

Paper: Somatosensory coding of visual self-identity.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2025.121405
Abstract: How does the brain process our bodily identity? This question has long fascinated scientists because of its potential implications for the study of self-awareness. Here, to test the idea that the somatosensory system is directly involved in coding bodily self-identity even when conveyed through vision, we probed the somatosensory system with tactile stimuli while participants observed hand images, either belonging to them (self-hand) or to another person (other-hand).
